words.meaning: to choose someone for a position by voting
words.example: Students will elect a class leader tomorrow.

words.meaning: to do what a rule or person tells you to do
to succeed in doing something after effort
words.example: She achieved her goal after months of practice.

words.meaning: to learn a skill or subject very well
words.example: It takes practice to master a new language.

words.meaning: to get information or an object back
words.japaneseMeaning: 取り戻す、検索して取り出す
words.example: The system can retrieve old emails quickly.

words.meaning: to say that someone or something is good
words.example: The teacher praised her careful work.

words.meaning: to make something longer or last longer
words.example: They will extend the deadline by one week.

words.meaning: to do a task, job, or action
words.example: The team performed the task carefully.

words.meaning: to improve something by making it newer or better
words.japaneseMeaning: 改良する、アップグレードする
words.example: The school upgraded its computer room.

words.meaning: to give someone a task or responsibility
words.example: The teacher assigned each group a topic.
